question.content: Given the FET circuit and output characteristic curves, identify key operating points and obtain the equivalent resistance in the variable-resistance region when ui=10V. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Use ASCII characters only. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Read the circuit topology: source is grounded, drain has load resistor R4 to +15V, and output is drain voltage.
step_1.key_point: Use drain current and load relation to map ui to operating point.

step_3.content: From the 10V characteristic, the variable-resistance segment is near-linear through origin, so rds uses UDS/ID on that line.
step_3.key_point: Using points around (3V,1mA) or (6V,2mA) gives rds=3000Ω.
